—The tribes were to camp around the tabernacle, which was to be in the middle of them. It was a sign of God’s gracious presence. Yet they were to pitch their tents far away, out of reverence for the sanctuary. The children of Israel took their positions without complaining or arguing; and as it was their safety, so it was their beauty. It is our duty and interest to be content with the place given to us, and to try to occupy it properly, without envying or complaining; without ambition or greed. Thus the gospel church ought to be united, according to the scriptural model, everyone knowing and keeping his place; and then all who wish well for the church will rejoice, seeing their order.
